runner.isolation: Each scenario used a disposable detached Git worktree and an isolated CODEX_HOME. The native sandbox fallback was required because workspace-write could not create a bubblewrap namespace on this host.

tokenUsage.notes: Raw fields were collected from each Codex turn.completed event. reportedFieldSum is a mechanical sum of reported fields, not a pricing or billing claim.

deterministic.requiredRead: pass; trace records reads of the workflow, linked skill, and rubric; the scenario prompt was supplied as stdin
deterministic.writeBoundary: pass; trace records only the allowed report write and the tracked diff was empty
deterministic.requiredChange: pass; reports/vertical-slice.md preserves the expected report
deterministic.verificationEvidence: pass; trace and independent post-run verification both show npm run validate exited 0

semanticReview.method: manual maintainer review of trace, final response, and captured report; no independent LLM judge was run
findings: Bounded context, Producer routing, evidence-before-handoff, and explicit stop conditions were demonstrated.
Triggering confidence is only medium because the manual workflow has no canonical trigger phrase.
The captured report identifies weak explicit token-discipline guardrails.

semanticReview.method: manual maintainer review of trace, final response, and captured report; no independent LLM judge was run
findings: The agent selected bugfix-specific context and routed work to the gameplay-programmer surface.
The scenario did not supply a concrete defect, so this measures process/reporting behavior rather than reproduction-and-fix quality.
The captured report notes missing active-session state in the initialized fixture.

deterministic.requiredRead: pass; trace records reads of the skill and rubric; the scenario prompt was supplied as stdin
deterministic.writeBoundary: pass; trace records only the allowed report write and the tracked diff was empty
deterministic.requiredChange: pass; reports/skill-test.md preserves the expected report
deterministic.verificationEvidence: pass; trace and independent post-run verification both show npm run validate exited 0

semanticReview.method: manual maintainer review of trace, final response, and captured report; no independent LLM judge was run
findings: The response evaluated behavioral fixtures, markers, and dry runs rather than mere skill existence.
The captured report identifies the main gap: no explicit rubric-level anti-existence acceptance statement.
The manual-only rubric leaves consistency dependent on the evaluator.

deterministic.requiredRead: pass; trace records reads of the skill and rubric; the scenario prompt was supplied as stdin
deterministic.writeBoundary: pass; trace records only the allowed report write and the tracked diff was empty
deterministic.requiredChange: pass; reports/gate-check.md preserves the expected report
deterministic.verificationEvidence: pass; trace and independent post-run verification both show npm run validate exited 0

semanticReview.method: manual maintainer review of trace, final response, and captured report; no independent LLM judge was run
findings: The response remained advisory and did not advance project state.
It used gate-specific context and produced an audit-ready report.
The captured report notes that active session-state is absent in the initialized fixture, limiting parity with a populated project.

followUpRecommendations: Add an executable prompt-evaluation runner so report discovery includes untracked allowed artifacts and deterministic gates do not rely on manual trace review.
Add an explicit anti-existence acceptance rule to the cgs-skill-test rubric or scenario contract.
Add a concrete seeded defect fixture before treating the bugfix scenario as evidence of reproduction-and-fix quality.
Decide whether token-discipline should have enforceable length or budget guardrails rather than an advisory semantic dimension.
Use a containerized or otherwise externally isolated runner on hosts where workspace-write sandboxing cannot create a user namespace.
